title: Technology Briefing: I avoid AI tools because thinking is supposed to be hard. It’s what makes us human | Wendy ...
summary: Leo Stone turns the latest technology headlines into an operator's view of platforms, infrastructure, and product strategy.
imageAlt: Image from The Guardian for I avoid AI tools because thinking is supposed to be hard. It’s what makes us human | Wendy Liu - The Guardian
imageCaption: I avoid AI tools because thinking is supposed to be hard. It’s what makes us human | Wendy Liu - The Guardian (The Guardian)

body.text: The useful way to read technology news is to ask what changes for builders and operators: platform control, infrastructure cost, data boundaries, security exposure, and product leverage.

items: I avoid AI tools because thinking is supposed to be hard. It’s what makes us human | Wendy Liu - The Guardian (The Guardian)
Venture Capitalist John Doerr Says AI Is the Biggest Tech ‘Tsunami’ Ever - WSJ (WSJ)
Gemini for Science: AI experiments and tools for a new era of discovery - blog.google (blog.google)
Pope Leo will take on AI alongside an Anthropic co-founder - NBC News (NBC News)
What Nvidia’s CFO Just Revealed About GPU Demand Should Have Every AI Investor Paying Attention - Yahoo Finance (Yahoo Finance)
AI agents are quietly generating chaos engineering failures enterprises don’t track yet - VentureBeat (VentureBeat)

body.text: The first-order story is rarely enough. A professional reading asks whether the news changes deployment risk, developer behavior, enterprise buying cycles, cloud economics, or the trust boundary around automation.
The strongest technical teams will treat these signals as prompts for better architecture decisions, not as reasons to chase every fashionable tool.
